findings.detail: Effective tax rate spiked to 24.09% in FY2024 from 14.72% in FY2023, then reverted to 15.61% in FY2025. This 2.3× increase represents approximately $11B in additional tax expense relative to a normalized rate. Likely reflects one-time tax items, repatriation of foreign earnings, or tax law changes, but should be verified against 10-K tax footnotes and MD&A to confirm no data extraction error.

OCF/NI fell to 1.00 in FY2025, the lowest in the dataset, from 1.26 in FY2024. Driven by OCF declining 5.8% YoY while NI grew 19.5%. Quarter-by-quarter, FY2025 Q1–Q2 show OCF below NI (0.82x, 0.97x)—unusual for Apple—while Q3–Q4 recover to normal (1.19x, 1.08x). Pattern likely reflects working capital timing, tax or dividend payment seasonality, but is worth monitoring for sustained divergence.
